research.product: VMware Cloud Foundation SDDC Manager (upgrade prechecks / check-sets, upgradables, tasks)
research.interface: SDDC Manager REST API 9.1 (developer.broadcom.com xAPIs, /v1 base path)

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Operation Index
official_sources.used_for: current operation surface: /v1/system/check-sets (queries, trigger run, get result), GET /v1/system/upgradables, GET /v1/tasks/{id}; confirmation that POST /v1/system/prechecks no longer exists in 9.x and that /v1/upgrades/{upgradeId}/prechecks is marked Deprecated

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Query for check-sets (POST /v1/system/check-sets/queries)
official_sources.used_for: CheckSetQueryInput shape {checkSetType, domains:[{domainId, resources:[{resourceType, resourceId, resourceTargetVersion}]}]} and CheckSetQueryResult {queryId, resources:[... domain{domainId,domainName,domainType}, checkSets:[{checkSetId,checkSetName,checkSetType}]]}, 200 response

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Trigger a run of the selected checks (POST /v1/system/check-sets)
official_sources.used_for: run input {queryId (required), resources (required, each with checkSets:[{checkSetId}]), metadata:{targetVersion, upgradeId}}; 202 Accepted returning a Task; Task status enum PENDING/IN_PROGRESS/SUCCESSFUL/FAILED/CANCELLED/COMPLETED_WITH_WARNING/SKIPPED/QUEUED/TIMED_OUT

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Get the result for a given check run (GET /v1/system/check-sets/{runId})
official_sources.used_for: runId is 'UUID of the task'; AssessmentOutput fields (status, validationResult, presentedArtifactsMap, discoveryProgress, timestamp, completionTimestamp) and status enum IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED_WITH_SUCCESS/COMPLETED_WITH_FAILURE

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Fetches the list of Upgradables in the System (GET /v1/system/upgradables)
official_sources.used_for: PageOfUpgradable {elements:[{bundleId, bundleType, resource, softwareComponents, status, errors}], pageMetadata}; 'Only one Upgradable becomes AVAILABLE for Upgrade'; 409 when the inventory cache is not seeded

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Get a Task by ID (GET /v1/tasks/{id})
official_sources.used_for: Task schema (id, name, type, status, creationTimestamp, completionTimestamp, subTasks, errors, resources, resolutionStatus, isCancellable, isRetryable) and the recursive Error schema (errorCode, errorType, message, remediationMessage, referenceToken, causes, nestedErrors); 404/500 responses

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Resource data structure
official_sources.used_for: current Resource fields are resourceId (required), fqdn, type (required enum), name, sans; the legacy 'address' field from older VCF API releases is absent, so resource descriptors carrying 'address' are refused in favor of 'fqdn'

official_sources.title: VMware Cloud Foundation API 5.2 - System Prechecks APIs
official_sources.used_for: historical context only: POST /v1/system/prechecks and GET /v1/system/prechecks/tasks/{id} are already marked Deprecated in the 5.2 documentation, corroborating that check-sets are the current upgrade-precheck surface

research.language_rationale: Broadcom publishes no official Python SDK for the SDDC Manager API; the documented integration surface is plain REST over /v1 with bearer tokens, and Python's stdlib urllib is the idiomatic zero-dependency way to drive it from upgrade-orchestration jobs.

verified_facts: In SDDC Manager 9.x the upgrade precheck surface is the check-sets workflow: POST /v1/system/check-sets/queries to discover applicable check-sets, POST /v1/system/check-sets to trigger a run (202 + Task), GET /v1/tasks/{id} to follow the task, GET /v1/system/check-sets/{runId} for the assessment result.
POST /v1/system/prechecks is deprecated in VCF 5.2 documentation and absent from the SDDC Manager 9.1 operation index; /v1/upgrades/{upgradeId}/prechecks is marked Deprecated in 9.1.
CheckSetQueryInput nests resources under domains: {checkSetType, domains:[{domainId, resources:[{resourceType, resourceId, resourceTargetVersion}]}]}.
The check-set run input requires queryId and resources (each resource carrying checkSets:[{checkSetId}]) and accepts metadata.targetVersion.
Task.status values: PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, SUCCESSFUL, FAILED, CANCELLED, COMPLETED_WITH_WARNING, SKIPPED, QUEUED, TIMED_OUT.
The VCF Error structure is recursive: errorCode, errorType, message, remediationMessage, referenceToken, causes, nestedErrors (list of Error).
GET /v1/system/upgradables returns PageOfUpgradable; only one Upgradable becomes AVAILABLE for upgrade at a time; 409 means the inventory cache is not seeded.
The 9.1 Resource model identifies resources by resourceId + fqdn; older releases' 'address' field no longer exists.
